really exerting any power either and so I'm up there telling them stuff they don't want to hear and I outsold everybody I mean I'm starting with telling them you know this internet thing is not what it's cracked up to be and they're at the underground internet marketing seminar and you know and they all look like you and I still outsell everybody a I know how to sell but B I looked appropriate so in the same vein your hijack 50 is a kind of encouraged assumption and that is that people make one plus one equals two calculations that are not true but they will easily make them and accept them because again what has been embedded in them since they were old enough to do any math one plus one equals two it's in there real deep hard to convince them one plus one can equal five for example real hard this is like tattooed in there so you can trigger it if you give them a one plus a one they will accept that those two things equal the two you show them all fake science is done this way in marketing so the one of the most famous of all time is is the pitch for shark cartilage supplements sharks don't get cancer there's one it happens to be true they don't sharks don't get cancer therefore if you eat shark cartilage you won't get cancer either the book sold mostly because was being used to sell pills but the book sold 12 14 million copies for a period of years 50 different until he drives him from office is the Kevin Spacey character manipulating the president often with two or three choices arguing both sides of each of these choices and guiding the president into doing what it is that the character's name is Frank Underwood what what he wants the president to do this is based on this is drawn from how Henry Kissinger reportedly managed Nixon so Kissinger says and it's in its it is I think it's third autobiography or his fourth autobiography it's there I ask him about it backstage and so Kissinger's contention which I have no reason to doubt is that Nixon really didn't know much about foreign policy and that Nixon was an incredibly insecure individual which I think probably is at this point self-evident so Kissinger knew what he wanted to do but if he just recommended that Nixon would automatically balk at it because he didn't want to let himself be pushed around so Kissinger would create two or three choices very carefully that he could argue both sides of but could position them in such a way that ultimately the only sensible choice was obvious to Nixon but Nixon could make it Nixon's idea occasionally Henry says he would argue vehemently for one that he knew Nixon would never do so that Nixon could really have his own idea one of the other two that Kissinger was opposed to you will see the same thing happening with the Underwood dynamic in House of Cards you will also we will often do this in sales and marketing with some sort of red herring choice sometimes it's just outrageously overpriced choice sometimes it's a horribly inadequate choice but we will have a red herring choice so that they can reject it and they can pick one of the others that are in front of that I have a client for a number of years who his main coaching program is $70,000 and we have a $300,000 red herring on top of it and his worst nightmare came true a year ago somebody took it and he said to deliver that thing which is not pleasant I can 12 minutes so this is your too young I'm gonna pick on you now instead of that okay I'd much rather look at them but I'm gonna pick on you so in 1938 many of you will know this and many of you will have heard it once but and I'm only gonna play 12 minutes but in 1938 radio was a new medium and people had radios in the house and they listened to new sports you listen to a boxing match you listen to baseball game you listen to drama and all of the news you got pretty much was on the radio and it was still a relatively new media so nobody was anywhere near as media savvy as people automatically are today and it was relatively easy to fool them now that has been repeated over and over and over again of course because TV infomercials in their early years were all about fooling people into thinking they were watching real TV and it was and it were that's why the Federal Trade Commission eventually they like pee on everything that's fun they eventually begin to chip away at this first by outlawing certain formats so like we can't spend a long time you can't do a TV infomercial with a news desk it cannot look like the CBS even news because they decided people can't tell it apart then they added all the announcements and disclaimers and paid advertisement and tell that stuff we have to do to a show to try and keep the public from thinking they're watching real television selling them something and they were right for the most part in the first handful of years most people were kind they did not know the difference between a real talk show and a fake talk show they did not know they didn't know the word infomercial now the public knows not only knows what these things are they know the word for them and we know this because they would say to us I watched your show they would use the same language in describing it as they used for real television so we succeeded at conning them even recently so each time there's an opportunity to fool them with media they get fooled and many people stay fooled long after they should not have been fooled it alternative formats at least suck people in so an advertorial in a magazine like it Forbes they make you put advertisement at the top of it but it still looks like an article and it's still in the same typeface as the articles and Forbes and it's got a picture of a famous person in it and it will often suck a reader in who would skip over an ad that looked like an ad and even though he's been told it's an ad it still gets him and now you have a better chance to get him interested in infomercial still work that way so most people now when they click on a Larry King look infomercial or a five women sitting around in a living room having a conversation sort of the view infomercial they mostly know or they know quick but it still stopped more of them than would a normal commercial and now you got a chance right but in the early days they were kind they didn't know the difference and therefore it was a lot easier to sell a lot of stuff when they totally didn't know the difference so in 1938 radio was a brand new medium nobody really was familiar with radio they were all experiencing it together as an American consensus of something very very new and it was relatively easy for horse and wells to do what he did he blurred the lines between fantasy and a news broadcast for a October 30th broadcast of War of it happened so there was this big setup before we get to the point that we are at now and so these 12 minutes then happen and there's a lot after it it just continues to get worse and worse and worse of course so here's trying imagine I was gonna say close your eyes and I decided against it because I don't want to run around waking people up so I actually so I actually had so I actually had Ron show you just to give you something to look at while you are listening okay because I don't trust you but do your best to imagine it's 1938 you have had a radio in your house for a relatively short period of time you have quickly come to understand certain formats there's news there's dramas there's comedies and there's variety shows pretty much radio and so you're becoming familiar with what this media is all about and now you have been listening to this dance music at Times Square kind of broadcast and you are now being interrupted with this build of news and think about how much fear this could engender in people you want to play the 12 ladies and gentlemen here is the latest bulletin from the Intercontinental Radio News Toronto Canada professor Morris of McMillan University reports observing a total of three explosions on the planet Mars between the hours of 7 45 p.m. and 9 20 p.m. Eastern Standard Time this confirms earlier reports from American observatories now nearer home comes a special bulletin from Trenton New Jersey it is reported that at 8 50 p.m. a huge flaming object believed to be a meteorite fell on a farm in the neighborhood of Grover's Mill New Jersey 22 miles from Trenton the flash in the sky was visible within a radius of several hundred miles and the noise of the impact was heard as far north as Elizabeth we have dispatched a special mobile unit to the scene and we'll have our commentator Carl Phillips give you a word picture of the scene as soon as he can reach there from Princeton in the meantime we take you to the Hotel Martinette in Brooklyn where Bobby Millett and his orchestra are offering a program of dance music. We take you now to Grover's Mill New Jersey. Ladies and gentlemen this is Carl Phillips again out at the Wilmwood farm Grover's Mill New Jersey. Specialist and myself made the 11 miles in Princeton in 10 minutes. Well I hardly know where to begin. Paint for you a word picture of the same thing before my eyes or something out of a modern Arabian night. Just got here I haven't had a chance to look around yet. I guess that's it yes I guess that's the thing directly in front of me. Half buried in a vast pit must have stuck with terrific force the ground is covered with sprinters of a tree it must have struck on its way down but I can see the object itself doesn't look very much like a meteor. At least not the meteors I've seen it looks more like a huge cylinder as a diameter of them. What would you say Professor Pearson. What would you say what's the diameter of the 30 yards about 30 yards the metal on the sheet is well I've never seen anything like it the color is sort of yellowish white curious spectators now are pressing close to the object in spite of the effort of the police to keep them back. Getting in front of my line of view would you mind standing one side please while it was. Here's Mr Wilmets owner of the farm here he may have some interesting facts to add Mr Wilmets would you please tell the radio audience as much as you remember of this rather unusual visitor that dropped in your backyard a step closer please ladies and gentlemen this is Mr Wilmets. I was listening to the radio. Close and louder please. Pardon me. A louder please. Close. I was listening to the radio and kind of drowsy. A professor fellow was talking about Mars. So I was half chosen. Yes yes this woman and then what happened. Well as I was saying I was listening to the radio kind of halfway. Yes Mr Wilmets and then you saw something. Not first off I heard something and what did you hear. Hissing sound like this. Kind of like a force that you lie rockin. Yes then what I turned my head out the window and would have sworn I was asleep and dreaming yes I think that kind of greenish streak and then Zingo something smack the ground knocked me clear out of my chair well where you frightened Mr Wilmets well I ain't quite sure I reckon I was kind of riled. Thank you Mr Wilmets thank you very much. That's quite all right that's plenty ladies and gentlemen you've just heard Mr Wilmets owner of the farm where this thing has fallen. I wish I could convey the atmosphere the background of this fantastic scene. Hundreds of cars have parked in the field in back of us and the police are trying to rope off the roadway leading into the farm but it's no use breaking right through cars headlights throwing an enormous spotlight on the pit where the objects have buried some of the more daring goings now are venturing near the edge of the city which stand out against the mental change. One man wants to touch the things having an argument with the police. Now the police and when now ladies and gentlemen there's something I haven't mentioned in all this excitement but it's becoming more distinct perhaps you've caught it already on your radio listen please. You hear the curious humming sound that seems to come from inside the object I'll move the microphone nearer here. Now we're not more than 25 feet away can you hear it now. Professor Pearson. Yes. Can you tell us the meaning of that scraping noise inside the thing. Possibly the unequal cooling of its surface I say do you still think it's a meteor professor. What do you think. Metal casing is definitely extra terrestrial not found on the surface. Friction with the earth that must be usually tears holes in a meteorite this thing is. See if something happening ladies and gentlemen this is. This end of the thing is beginning to flake off the. Office beginning to rotate like a screw in this thing must be hollow. Keep going. Move it. It's all. Ladies and gentlemen this is the most terrifying thing I've ever witnessed. Wait a minute. Someone calling someone or something I can see. Going out of that black hole to luminous discs. The eyes might be a face might be on the. Heavens something like a gray snake now it's another one and another one and another one. They look like tentacles to me that. I can see the things body now it's large large the bear. This is like wet leather but. Ladies and gentlemen to describe it like I hardly force myself to keep looking at the whole point eyes are black and the green like a serpent the mountain is kind of the shape was alive and dripping from the swimless lips and seem to. Those quivered pulsating monster or whatever it is can hardly move it seems weighed down by their possibly gravity or something with things rising up down the crowd falls back to seem plenty that. Was the start of their experience with you don't like their fine words and well I pull this microphone with me as I talk I have to stop the description till I can take a new position on what you please I'll be right back in a minute. We are bringing you and I witness the count of what's happening on the Wilmuth farm Groversville New Jersey. We now return you to call Phillips at Groversville. Ladies and gentlemen my own ladies and gentlemen ladies and gentlemen here I am back of the stone wall of the joint Mr Wilmuth's garden from here I get a sweep of the whole scene I'll give you every detail as long as I can talk and as long as I can see more state police have arrived at drawing up a cordon in front of the pit about 30 of them. No need to push the card back now they're willing to keep their distance the captain's inferring with someone can't quite see who I believe it's Professor Pearson. Yes it is now they've parted and the professor moves around one side studying the object while the captain and two policemen advanced with something in their hands I can see it now it's a white hex if I do a poll. Like a truce those preachers know what that means. But anything means something's happening. Shape is rising out of the pit. They make out a small beam of light against a mirror. The jet of flame is in the mirror at least right at the magic man. Starts him head on. They're turning in the plane. The whole field is caught up by the woods as far as the gas is hitting everywhere coming this way now. Ladies and gentlemen due to circumstances beyond our control we are unable to continue the broadcast from Grover's mill evidently there's some difficulty with our field transmission. However we will return to that point at the earliest opportunity. In the meantime we have a late bulletin from San Diego California. Professor Indelkofer speaking at the moment is a very important point. I'm not sure if you've heard of it or not. I'm not sure. We continue now with our piano interlude. Ladies and gentlemen I've just been handed a message that came in from Grover's mill by telephone. Just one moment please. At least 40 people including six state troopers lie dead in a field east of the village of Grover's mill. Their bodies burned and distorted beyond all possible recognition. The next voice you hear will be that of Brigadier General Montgomery Smith, commander of the state militia at Trenton, New Jersey. I have been requested by the governor of New Jersey to place the counties of Mercer and Middlesex as far west as Princeton and east to Jamesburg under martial law. No one will be permitted to enter this area except by special pass issued by state or military authorities. Four companies of state militia are proceeding from Trenton to Grover's mill and will aid in the evacuation of homes within the range of military operations. Thank you. You have just been listening to General Montgomery Smith commanding the state militia at Trenton. In the meantime further details of the catastrophe at Grover's mill are coming in. The strange creatures after unleashing their deadly assault crawl back in their pit and made no attempt to prevent the efforts of the firemen to recover the bodies and extinguish the fire. The combined fire departments of Mercer County are fighting the flames which menace the entire countryside. We have been unable to establish any contact with our mobile unit at Grover's mill, but we hope to be able to return you there at the earliest possible moment. In the meantime we take you to just one moment please.
